Version 2 — adds CI findings

What's new in v2

This v2 report adds 12 new findings surfaced by the latest CI/CD analysis (Parallel Full Suite + Parallel Suite 2 runs). The new entries are BUG-21 through BUG-32, including 2 HIGH-severity security findings (BUG-08 Auth cookies SameSite=None / CSRF risk and BUG-09 No login rate limiting). The Critical Path data-integrity cluster is now expanded to include work-order count mismatch (BUG-21), KPI formatting regressions (BUG-22, BUG-23), and additional navigation/session bugs (BUG-25, BUG-26).

Findings

BUG-01 HIGH

Kebab menu does not close on ESC, outside-click, or toggle

Surface/assets/ (asset detail page)
Caught byTC_BH_13_KebabMenuClosesOnOutsideClick

Description

The asset detail page kebab menu (Edit Asset / Delete Asset options) cannot be dismissed by any standard mechanism. Focus trap. Violates WAI-ARIA menu pattern.

Steps to reproduce

  1. Navigate to /assets and log in.
  2. Click any row in the assets grid to open detail page.
  3. Click the three-dot kebab icon (top-right of detail header).
  4. Verify menu opens with "Edit Asset" / "Delete Asset" items.
  5. Press ESC key.
  6. Click on empty area of the page.
  7. Click kebab icon again (toggle attempt).

Expected behavior

ESC + outside-click + toggle should each close the menu (WAI-ARIA + MUI defaults).

Actual behavior

Menu remains open after ALL three close methods. User stuck.

Impact

Severe accessibility violation. Keyboard / screen-reader users blocked.

Captured during automated test failure.
BUG-02 HIGH

Uncaught TypeError: r?.hasAttribute is not a function (5x per page)

Surface/assets — JavaScript runtime error
Caught byTC_BH_12_AssetsPageNoConsoleErrors

Description

A JavaScript TypeError fires 5 times per /assets page load. Optional chaining (r?.) protects null/undefined but not against wrong-type values (Text node treated as Element).

Steps to reproduce

  1. Open browser DevTools Console.
  2. Clear console.
  3. Navigate to https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai/assets
  4. Wait 5 seconds for full load.

Expected behavior

Zero JavaScript errors at SEVERE level.

Actual behavior

5 SEVERE errors: "Uncaught TypeError: r?.hasAttribute is not a function" from index-3RELFuA2.js:270:219332

Impact

React error boundaries swallow but indicates broken DOM-traversal code.

[No screenshot — bug is observable via DevTools console / network tab]

BUG-03 HIGH

Dashboard asset count does not match asset list count

Surface/dashboard/site-overview vs /assets
Caught byCriticalPathTestNG.testCP_DI_001_AssetCountMatchesDashboard

Description

Site Overview dashboard reports asset count that does not match underlying asset list. Stale cache, tenant-isolation breakage, or different data sources between widget and module page.

Steps to reproduce

  1. Log in to https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai
  2. Navigate to Site Overview / Dashboard.
  3. Note asset count in KPI card.
  4. Navigate to /assets module.
  5. Note total in pagination footer ("of N").

Expected behavior

Counts match exactly (single source of truth).

Actual behavior

Dashboard reports 1,953 assets; /assets grid reports 8,429 (delta +6,476).

Impact

Customer cannot trust either number.

Captured during automated test failure.
BUG-04 HIGH

Dashboard task count does not match Tasks module

Surface/dashboard vs /tasks
Caught byCriticalPathTestNG.testCP_DI_002_TaskCountMatchesDashboard

Description

Same data-integrity class as BUG-03.

Steps to reproduce

  1. Log in to https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai
  2. Navigate to Site Overview / Dashboard.
  3. Note "Pending Tasks" KPI count.
  4. Navigate to /tasks module.
  5. Note total count.

Expected behavior

Counts match.

Actual behavior

Dashboard reports 147 pending tasks; Tasks module reports 0.

Impact

Workflow ambiguity — user cannot reconcile.

Captured during automated test failure.
BUG-05 HIGH

Dashboard issue count does not match Issues module

Surface/dashboard vs /issues
Caught byCriticalPathTestNG.testCP_DI_003_IssueCountMatchesDashboard

Description

Same data-integrity class.

Steps to reproduce

  1. Log in.
  2. Navigate to Dashboard. Note "Unresolved Issues" KPI.
  3. Navigate to /issues module.

Expected behavior

Counts match.

Actual behavior

Dashboard reports 266; Issues grid reports 0.

Impact

Customer-visible data inconsistency.

Captured during automated test failure.
BUG-06 HIGH

Asset grid is empty after page refresh

Surface/assets — state recovery on refresh
Caught byCriticalPathTestNG.testCP_DI_005_AssetDataSurvivesRefresh

Description

After /assets renders, pressing F5 results in an empty grid.

Steps to reproduce

  1. Log in.
  2. Navigate to /assets and wait for grid to render.
  3. Press F5 to refresh.
  4. Wait 5+ seconds.

Expected behavior

Grid re-fetches and displays the same rows.

Actual behavior

Grid renders empty — no clickable rows.

Impact

F5 should be a safe operation. Users cannot reliably refresh.

Captured during automated test failure.
BUG-07 HIGH

Beamer widget URL leaks user PII (role / company / name) in query params

SurfaceBeamer notification widget URL
Caught byBugHuntGlobalTestNG.testBUG018_BeamerLeaksUserData

Description

Beamer URL contains user PII as query parameters (role, company, name). URLs are routinely logged at proxies / CDNs.

Steps to reproduce

  1. Log in.
  2. Open DevTools Network tab.
  3. Filter for "beamer".
  4. Inspect any Beamer URL.

Expected behavior

PII passed via secure POST body or HTTP-only cookie, never URL params.

Actual behavior

URL contains role=true, company=true, name=true (user identity leaked).

Impact

GDPR / privacy concern. Logs at CDN, proxies, analytics.

Captured during automated test failure.
BUG-08 HIGH

Auth cookies use SameSite=None (CSRF risk) [SECURITY]

SurfaceAuthentication — set-cookie headers
Caught bySecurityAuditTestNG.testBUG007_AuthCookiesSameSiteNone

Description

Browser set-cookie headers for the authentication cookies use SameSite=None. This allows the cookies to be sent on cross-site requests, opening up CSRF attack vectors.

Steps to reproduce

  1. Open DevTools Network tab.
  2. Log in to https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai
  3. Inspect the response headers of the login response.
  4. Look at Set-Cookie header for the auth/session cookie.
  5. Check the SameSite attribute.

Expected behavior

Auth cookies should use SameSite=Lax (default modern web) or SameSite=Strict for max protection.

Actual behavior

Auth cookies set with SameSite=None — allows cross-site sending.

Impact

CSRF (Cross-Site Request Forgery) vulnerability. An attacker could forge requests on behalf of an authenticated user from a malicious site. Block-on-ship security finding.

[No screenshot — bug is observable via DevTools console / network tab]

BUG-09 HIGH

No rate limiting on login endpoint [SECURITY]

Surface/api/auth/login — rate limit
Caught bySecurityAuditTestNG.testBUG008_NoRateLimiting

Description

The login endpoint accepts unlimited rapid-fire requests with no throttling, lockout, or rate limiting. Allows credential-stuffing and brute-force attacks.

Steps to reproduce

  1. Open DevTools Network tab.
  2. Send POST requests to /api/auth/login with intentionally wrong credentials.
  3. Send 50+ requests in quick succession.
  4. Observe each response.

Expected behavior

After N consecutive failures (typically 5-10), the endpoint should return 429 Too Many Requests OR lock the account temporarily.

Actual behavior

All requests get processed and return the same 401 / 400 — no rate limiting kicks in.

Impact

Brute-force / credential-stuffing attacks unhindered. Combined with no MFA = serious security exposure.

[No screenshot — bug is observable via DevTools console / network tab]

BUG-10 HIGH

KPI cards missing on dashboard (0 cards rendered)

Surface/dashboard / Site Overview
Caught byCriticalPathTestNG.testCP_FA_003_KPINumbersNonNegative

Description

Dashboard renders zero KPI cards. The widgets that should show counts/metrics are missing or failing to render.

Steps to reproduce

  1. Log in.
  2. Navigate to Site Overview / Dashboard.
  3. Wait for full page load (5+ seconds).
  4. Count visible KPI cards.

Expected behavior

≥4 KPI cards visible (Equipment at Risk, Opportunities, Pending Tasks, Unresolved Issues, etc.).

Actual behavior

0 KPI cards rendered.

Impact

Dashboard is the homepage — without KPIs it is useless.

Captured during automated test failure.
BUG-11 MEDIUM

/api/auth/v2/me returns 401 on every page load

SurfaceAuthentication bootstrap
Caught byTC_BH_12_AssetsPageNoConsoleErrors (network monitor)

Description

On every page load, /api/auth/v2/me returns HTTP 401. App recovers via fallback path, but the failed call pollutes browser console as a SEVERE error.

Steps to reproduce

  1. Open DevTools Network tab.
  2. Navigate to https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai
  3. Filter for "auth/v2".
  4. Note response status of /me.

Expected behavior

/api/auth/v2/me returns 200 OK with user info.

Actual behavior

401 Unauthorized. Likely token-bootstrap race.

Impact

Console pollution; backend log noise; slow page boot due to retry path.

[No screenshot — bug is observable via DevTools console / network tab]

BUG-12 MEDIUM

/api/auth/v2/refresh returns 400 on every page load

SurfaceAuthentication refresh
Caught byTC_BH_12_AssetsPageNoConsoleErrors

Description

Token refresh endpoint returns HTTP 400 on every page load.

Steps to reproduce

  1. Open DevTools Network tab.
  2. Navigate to https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai
  3. Filter for "auth/v2/refresh".

Expected behavior

Refresh returns 200 OK or is not called when not needed.

Actual behavior

Returns 400 Bad Request on every page load.

Impact

Wasted backend cycles; console pollution.

[No screenshot — bug is observable via DevTools console / network tab]

BUG-13 MEDIUM

PLuG (DevRev support widget) authentication initialization fails

Surface/assets — third-party PLuG widget
Caught byTC_BH_12_AssetsPageNoConsoleErrors

Description

PLuG widget logs "Error initializing with authentication" three times per /assets page load.

Steps to reproduce

  1. Open DevTools Console.
  2. Navigate to https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai/assets

Expected behavior

PLuG widget initializes silently or with single info log.

Actual behavior

3 SEVERE errors: "[PLuG] Error initializing with authentication:
